title: guzmania andreana
intro: Guzmania andreana, also known as the Queen of the Andes, is a species of epiphytic flowering plant in the family Bromeliaceae. It has been used in traditional medicine in South America for its medicinal properties, including antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effects, which have been attributed to its high concentration of saponins and flavonoids. The plant is believed to provide various health benefits, including reducing fever, treating respiratory issues, and alleviating digestive problems. Guzmania andreana is often used in herbal preparations such as teas and infusions, which are typically made by steeping the leaves and flowers in hot water.
